subject: Re: WarpTools is awesome, but I miss the GUI for checking CTF fits
body:
Have you tried
WarpTools filter_quality --settings warp_frameseries.settings --histograms
?
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK


On Wed, 28 Aug 2024 at 16:45, 'Lorenz Grundmann' via Warp <
warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:

> Hi Jesse,
>
> I also would love such a functionality. Did you find a workaround to
> display the CTF estimation results?
>
> Cheers,
> Lorenz
>
> Jesse Hansen schrieb am Freitag, 19. Juli 2024 um 12:41:43 UTC+2:
>
>> hi,
>>
>> thanks so much for WarpTools.  It's phenomenal.  Definitely a game
>> changer for our pipelines.
>>
>> It seems like it's reasonable to use command line for all features of
>> this processing, except perhaps one point I can think of.  When I check the
>> CTF fits of the tilt series I sometimes have to play around a bit with the
>> CTF fitting params, process for a single TS, check the fit, and repeat as
>> needed.  Do you have a suggestion on how to circumvent this need?  Perhaps
>> is it possible to view to CTF estimation results in the windows GUI then
>> continue processing in WarpTools?
>>
>> thanks again!
>>
>> best
>>
>> Jesse
>>
>>
>> --
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
